title: Tool Design, Errors, Pagination, and Context Efficiency · Skill Foundry
summary: Refine an MCP server whose tools are ambiguous and return too much data. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of an efficient tool API and evaluation suite while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose tool granularity that maps to meaningful user intent without unsafe generic execution.
Write discriminative descriptions, constrained schemas, examples, and side-effect metadata.
Return actionable domain errors without exposing secrets or stack internals.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append a agent-ext-tool-evals-vbsys2: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Tool Evals into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
agent-ext-tool-evals-vbsys2:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

stimulus.title: Tool Evals failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Handler unit tests measure agent usability.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate selection, argument construction, recovery, context cost, and side-effect accuracy.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Tool Evals condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Tool Evals applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Evaluate selection, argument construction, recovery, context cost, and side-effect accuracy.
